quoteFirst: This would not be fair to the other watch; and to avoid this repetition, and to divide the time differently each day, the hours from four to eight in the afternoon are divided up into what are called _dog-watches_ of two hours each, which breaks up the daily regularity, and changes the hours; so that the starboard watch who happen to be on deck from
quoteLast: one day are the next day below during the same hours, and the port watch on deck; and thus the same watches come round every forty-eight hours.
title: Practical Boat-Sailing: A Concise and Simple Treatise
author: Frazar, Douglas, 1836-1896
